title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Title: Can a Grandfather Gift an Insurance Policy to a Grandchild in India?
Alt Title: Grandparent gifting insurance policy to grandchild in India: Here is all you need to know!
Being a grandparent is the most rewarding experience in one’s life. There are many ways to express your love and adulation. One of the most rewarding and meaningful gifts that you can extend to your grandchild is an insurance policy. Here are the nuances that you should be aware of whilst buying insurance for your grandchild in India.
Who can buy insurance for grandchildren in India?
Any grandparent with an insurable interest can gift an insurance policy for their grandchild. Based on prior consent from the child's parents, one can gift the insurance at any given point.
The insurance so gifted can be a child plan which offers corpus for the child’s education, or any other type of insurance policy wherein the grandchildren are declared as dependents. The policyholder will be the grandparent and hence, responsible for premium payments. The grandparent could be the legal guardian of the child as well. Even the tax benefits can be availed by the one who is paying the premium, i.e. the proposer of the plan.Upon the death of a grandparent (if the cover is on them), the grandchild will receive the death benefit under the plan. Alternatively, many plans offer to cover the minor child. In such case, upon the untimely demise of the child, the grandparent or the beneficiary named in the policy will get the death benefit.
It is ideal to purchase a child plan which offers corpus at appropriate intervals, in line with their milestones. This will ensure that they can pursue their dreams without struggling with finances.
Key aspects to consider while buying insurance for grandchildren in India:
Insurable interest: The insurance company insists on the demonstration of insurable interest. If the minor child is being insured, then you need to have a justifiable reason. If you are the sole guardian for your grandchild and are currently raising her / him, then that could be a very valid reason. Another justifiable reason could be building enough corpus for your grandchild’s education.
Eligibility criteria: The eligibility criteria could vary depending on the type of insurance plan chosen. Child plans can be availed for a child between the age of 0 and 17 years. This is the standard age at entry. Children will be under the protection of insurance until they attain financial independence and become owners of the policy. Thereafter, they can also take over the premium payments.
Tenure of the policy: The tenure of the policy is dependent on the type of policy availed. If it is with a risk management objective, then a whole life policy will be undertaken. For savings and education funding, the maximum policy term would depend on the age of the grandchild.
Types of policy: Various types of insurance policies are available for grandparents to invest in, whole life insurance, ULIPs, money back plans, endowment plans etc. Child plans and ULIPs offer the most benefit in this respect. The equity exposure in equities helps create a higher corpus which could facilitate their education. As the proposer of the policy, you can discuss it with the parents of the child and choose the right insurance based on the financial objective that they have set for the child. You can either avail of a plan which provides periodic payouts to the child to align with their educational milestones or simply add them as the beneficiary to your life insurance policy. Since they may be minor beneficiaries, you may have to add a guardian alongside the minor beneficiary.
Affordable premiums: In all probability, you are probably nearing your retirement years or already retired. You must ensure that your golden years are not burdened with premium payments beyond your ability. You should ascertain the objective of purchasing insurance and the premiums by using online calculators. Ensure that it is economically feasible in the long run.
Medical Underwriting: Children fall under the low-risk category concerning insurance. There is often no requirement for medical tests before the insurance policy issuance. However, if the child has any medical complexities, the same has to be disclosed appropriately. Such cases may be subject to medical tests before the child takes up ownership of the policy. However, the underwriting norms can be quite strict if the life insured is the grandparent, especially with any pre-existing condition. So, for easy issuance of the plan, it is advisable to insure the life of the child. The maturity and survival benefit of the plan can be utilised for the benefit of the child.
Reasons for buying insurance for grandchildren in India:
There are many reasons why insurance makes sense. Here are some of them:
An ideal way to build funds for a child’s education and other long-term financial objectives of the child, such as marriage.
Maturity benefits accruing from money-back policies, endowment policies, ULIPs or any child plan would be tax-free in the hands of the recipient under section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961.
If a grandchild is a nominee, then under whole life insurance policies, the benefits would be paid out to the grandchild, and the benefits you receive upon the death of the insured are tax-free.
Buying insurance when the child is healthy ensures future insurability, even if the child were to develop any medical complications in the future.
Premiums for insurance plans where the child is the insured are low as the insured falls within the low-risk category.
Most child plans are accompanied by a built-in premium waiver benefit, wherein if the policyholder, i.e. the grandparent, dies within the policy tenure, the future premiums would be waived, and the policy would continue. The maturity benefit would be paid out as per the predefined schedule for the child’s financial future.
Buying a term plan with the child as the insured may seem like a futile effort as the child has no source of income and grandparents are not financially dependent on the child. It makes more sense to buy high-yielding insurance plans which are designed specifically to cater to their education and future expenses.
By gifting an insurance plan to your grandchild, you also iterate and teach them a lesson about the need for financial planning and security. Many conventional gifts lose value over time. This is one gift that will grow in value that they will cherish over the long haul.
